paragraphs: Prior to the 1945 renumbering, the route that became SR 1 had the following numbers:
SR 1 was defined in the 1945 renumbering as:
Since then, the following changes have been made:

The Jungle Trail (pre-1945 State Road 252) was part of A1A in northeastern Indian River County, Florida. The narrow, .mw-parser-output .frac{white-space:nowrap}.mw-parser-output .frac .num,.mw-parser-output .frac .den{font-size:80%;line-height:0;vertical-align:super}.mw-parser-output .frac .den{vertical-align:sub}.mw-parser-output .sr-only{border:0;clip:rect(0,0,0,0);clip-path:polygon(0px 0px,0px 0px,0px 0px);height:1px;margin:-1px;overflow:hidden;padding:0;position:absolute;width:1px}7+1⁄2-mile-long (12.1 km) road is located between Old Winter Beach Road and the current A1A, along the western side of Orchid Island, and is unpaved. It is part of the Indian River Lagoon Scenic Highway system, and the southernmost road in the highway system. Part of the trail goes through the Pelican Island National Wildlife Refuge.

gallery.desc: Florida State Road A1A : UNCONFIRMED ROAD ALIGNMENTS. These alignments have not been confirmed by the Florida Department of Transportation. They are based upon historical information provided in benchmarks of the National Geodetic Survey. Reports are provided on the recovery (or finding) the mark. They include driving directions which normally include local and state road names. Background is a co
gallery.credit: Mcmillen76 at English Wikipedia · Public domain

gallery.cap: A1A Scenic and Historic Coastal Byway Coquina Stone
gallery.desc: A detailed photo of the natural landscape features timeworn coquina stone, a popular choice in local buildings. Pressure applied over millions of years forms coquina from millions of seashells and small marine creatures. Location: Florida (29.863° N 81.270° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: A1A Scenic and Historic Coastal Byway Scenic A1A
gallery.desc: The sand dunes creep up to Highway A1A. Location: Florida (29.643° N 81.204° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by A. E. Crane
gallery.credit: A. E. Crane, U.S. Department of Transportation · Public domain

gallery.cap: A1A Scenic and Historic Coastal Byway The Low Slung A1A Bridge
gallery.desc: A bridge on A1A crosses the Matanzas River as seen from the ferry landing at Fort Matanzas National Monument . Location: (29.915° N 81.306° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by S. Clyde

gallery.cap: A1A Scenic and Historic Coastal Byway Yard Sale Shoppers on Florida's
gallery.desc: Shoppers peruse the merchandise on display at a stop along the A1A Super Scenic 72-mile Garage Sale. Location: Florida Status: Public domain.
